g.data-narration: Welcome! In this lecture, we'll explore Process Operations - the fundamental actions OS performs on processes.
A process is a program in execution - the fundamental unit of work in an operating system.
Process operations enable multitasking, allow programs to create child tasks, and manage system resources efficiently.
g.text: Allow programs to spawn child tasks

g.data-narration: Process creation instantiates a new process. A parent process creates a child process, forming a process tree hierarchy.
g.text: Parent process creates a child process → forms a process tree
In UNIX/Linux: All processes trace back to init (PID 1)
Reasons: User request, System boot-up, Batch jobs, System calls

g.data-narration: The fork() system call creates an exact copy of the parent process. It returns 0 to child and child's PID to parent.
g.text: Creates a child process as a duplicate of parent
Child gets a copy of parent's address space

g.data-narration: The exec() system call replaces the current process memory with a new program. The PID remains the same!
g.text: Replaces the current process memory space
Loads a new program from disk
PID remains the same, but program changes completely

g.data-narration: This diagram shows the complete flow: parent forks a child, child continues, then execs a new program while parent waits.
g.text: Process Creation Flow: fork() + exec() + wait()

g.data-narration: Process termination reclaims all resources. Reasons include normal exit, errors, fatal errors, or being killed by another process.
g.text: Steps: 1) Save status → 2) Deallocate resources → 3) Free PCB → 4) Notify parent via wait()

g.data-narration: A zombie is a terminated child whose parent hasn't called wait(). An orphan is a child whose parent terminated first.
The wait() system call suspends the parent until a child terminates. It retrieves exit status and prevents zombie processes.
g.text: Suspends parent execution until a child terminates
Retrieves child's exit status and allows OS to deallocate child's PCB
Prevents zombie processes - critical for resource cleanup

g.data-narration: Process operations cause state transitions: fork moves to Ready, exit moves to Terminated, wait moves to Waiting.
Interprocess Communication enables processes to cooperate. Two main models: shared memory (fast) and message passing (easier).
g.text: System calls: shmget(), shmat() | pipe(), msgget(), msgsnd(), msgrcv()

g.data-narration: Here's a quick reference table of key process operations and their system calls.
To summarize: fork() creates, exec() replaces, wait() synchronizes, and exit() terminates. Master these for exam success!
